items.title: This unheralded sector may be the one sure thing for bulls in July
items.description: The Nasdaq-100 is known for almost always rising in July, but REITs have done even better, as they remain undefeated since 2008.

items.title: Is Meta ‘giving up’ on cutting-edge AI? Wall Street is divided over potential cloud pivot.
items.description: Meta shares popped on reports of a new cloud-computing venture, but some analysts wonder if that would signal disappointing uptake of internal AI offerings.

items.title: ‘I don’t think I’ll make it to 80’: I’m 70 and single. Do I take out a reverse mortgage or a home-equity agreement?
‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.description: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: Older workers are fighting ageism by starting their own businesses — and they’re outperforming younger entrepreneurs
items.description: A business founder at age 50 is almost twice as likely to experience success as someone in their 30s.

items.title: CoreWeave, Nebius shares tumble as Meta stands to become a fresh threat in the cloud
items.description: Meta’s reported interest in monetizing its AI infrastructure is leading investors to question the sustainability of neocloud business models.

items.title: Is the U.S. jobs market getting better? June employment report to give us a heat check.
items.description: Is it getting easier to find a job? Are businesses really hiring more workers? That’s what the official data statistics tell us — but Americans aren’t so sure.

items.title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
items.description: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

items.title: Social Security’s reality check — with a side of wishful thinking
items.description: How demographics and recent tax laws are squeezing Social Security.

items.title: Sandisk’s and Micron’s stocks sink as the rotation trade builds, but supply shortages should limit losses
items.description: Most of Sandisk’s annual revenue could eventually come from its new business model contracts that provide better visibility, BofA said.
